title: Tradeshow inventory management board
excerpt: has anyone figured out a way to populate and organize your tradeshow inventory on monday.com? we are wanting to have 3 boards (furniture, decor and literature) and have specific columns to keep track of them but then hav…

posts.author: lcoleman (lauren coleman)
posts.cookedHtml: has anyone figured out a way to populate and organize your tradeshow inventory on monday.com? we are wanting to have 3 boards (furniture, decor and literature) and have specific columns to keep track of them but then have each product continually updated and connected by each tradeshow board? so that we can easily see the products being taken to each show.

posts.author: Rob_M (Robert Marringa)
posts.cookedHtml: @lcoleman did you ever figure this out? My teammate is in the same pickle currently. Have a good day.

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Rob_M you can try out the monday.com inventory app.

Inventory - monday.com Marketplace Inventory - monday.com Marketplace

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lcoleman @Rob_M

We’ve worked with teams that needed to track hundreds of items daily from medical equipment to catering tools and supplies to even golf course gear and vehicles, just to name a few.

Our app, QR Item Tracker in the monday.com marketplace, makes it easier since each item can be checked in/out with a quick scan, while logging user details, locations, and more. You can set it up to match your own workflow, whether that’s for furniture, office equipment, or shipments.

Here’s the product page with more details: QR Item Tracker

And a link to the Tutorial: Track Equipment and Assets with a QR Code in monday.com

If you’d like, we’d be happy to walk you through a free demo and help with setup.

Cheers,
Avo
